---
name: Response observation (anonymous protocol report)
about: Anonymous report of a rescue / maintenance / remission protocol you've found works for you. Compound + route + onset + duration. Patient-reported data is gold; no PHI.
title: '[response] '
labels: response-observation, patient-reported
---

> ⚠️ **Anonymous by design.** Do NOT include identifying information (name,
> location, clinician name, dates of care, medical-record content). We are
> capturing the **protocol pattern**, not you. If you submit anything that
> looks like PHI we will edit it out and ask you to re-confirm.
>
> ⚠️ **This is not medical advice.** We do not endorse any specific protocol.
> We aggregate patient-reported data so researchers and clinicians can see
> patterns worth investigating. **Do not change your own protocol based on
> what you read here.** Work with a mast-cell-knowledgeable clinician.

## Compound

- **Compound:** (e.g. Diphenhydramine / cetirizine / cromolyn / sulforaphane / quercetin / luteolin / …)
- **Approximate dose:** (mg / mL / dropperful — be honest about "approximately")
- **Form:** (tablet / capsule / liquid / sublingual / nasal / topical / injection / suppository / nebulized)

## Route of administration

- [ ] Oral — swallowed
- [ ] Buccal — held between cheek and gum
- [ ] Sublingual — dissolved under tongue
- [ ] Crushed + gummed (oral mucosal — same idea as buccal but crushed first)
- [ ] Nasal spray
- [ ] Inhaled / nebulized
- [ ] Subcutaneous (epi-pen-style)
- [ ] Intramuscular
- [ ] Intravenous (clinical setting)
- [ ] Topical / transdermal
- [ ] Rectal / suppository
- [ ] Other (describe):

## Use case

- [ ] **Rescue** — stopping an active reaction / flare
- [ ] **Pre-medication** — before a known trigger exposure
- [ ] **Maintenance** — daily / routine use to prevent flares
- [ ] **Remission attempt** — long-term upstream therapy
- [ ] Other:

## Effect pattern

- **Onset (after administration):** (minutes / hours)
- **Duration of effect:** (hours / no clear endpoint)
- **Symptoms it addresses:** (skin / GI / respiratory / neuro / cardiovascular — dominant domain, no specifics)
- **Magnitude of effect:** (subjective scale acceptable: stopped the reaction completely / blunted it / no effect / made it worse)

## What didn't work (optional but valuable)

If you've tried alternative compounds / routes / doses for the same use
case and they didn't give the same result, note them here. Comparative
data is especially useful.

## Suspected mechanism (optional)

If you have a hypothesis about *why* this works for you (BBB penetration?
fast absorption? specific receptor profile?), share it. Not required.

## Anything special about your case

- [ ] I have confirmed MCAS / MCAD diagnosis (no need to share details)
- [ ] I have suspected but unconfirmed MCAS
- [ ] I am a caregiver reporting on behalf of someone else (with their consent)
- [ ] I have a related condition (POTS / EDS / hEDS / long COVID / …) — no need to specify which
- [ ] None of the above

## What this report should NOT include

- Your name or any identifying information.
- Your clinician's name.
- Dates of care.
- Excerpts from your medical record.
- Specific exposure details that could be used to identify you.
- Photos / images of yourself or your reactions.

If we spot any of the above, we will edit the issue to remove it and
notify you via the issue thread.

## Why we ask

Patient-reported response data is one of the most underused signals in
MCAS research. Aggregated across many anonymous contributors, the
patterns this template captures (e.g. *"buccal first-gen H1 works in
~10 min for skin + respiratory reactions"* or *"luteolin + quercetin
combo blunts chemical-trigger flares within an hour"*) inform our
trigger-mechanism map, our rescue / maintenance / remission rankings,
and our experiment prioritization. Every submission goes through the
public-by-default review process — no embargoes, no patent capture.

---

Thank you. Your contribution stays anonymous.
